speaker: James Clear
episode_or_content_title: Atomic Habits: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones (Chapter 16) + jamesclear.com/second-mistake
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. James Clear is the author of Atomic Habits (New York Times #1 bestseller; 20M+ copies). The "never miss twice" concept appears in Chapter 16 of Atomic Habits and is elaborated in the official article at jamesclear.com/second-mistake. The full text of "Avoid the Second Mistake" was verified by fetching jamesclear.com/second-mistake directly. Key framing confirmed: "missing once is an accident, missing twice is the start of a new habit" appears as a direct quote on Clear's own official site (jamesclear.com/quotes) and in X posts he authored. The companion quote from Chapter 16 — "The first mistake is never the one that ruins you. It is the spiral of repeated mistakes that follows" — is confirmed on multiple official platforms (jamesclear.com, his official X account). The jamesclear.com/second-mistake article is an official canonical source.
